title: Codex changelog
author: OpenAI Developers
why_it_matters: Official baseline for Codex capability changes, including mobile and integration signals.
actionable_takeaway: Use this as the first daily source before reading X reactions.

title: OpenAI Agents SDK docs
author: OpenAI
why_it_matters: Shows the primitives needed to split the AI radar into multiple cooperating agents later.
actionable_takeaway: Model future pipeline as collector, evaluator, distiller, and action recommender agents.

title: Two kinds of scheduled work in Codex
author: Jason Liu
why_it_matters: Directly maps to the user's daily X learning and knowledge-base mechanism.
actionable_takeaway: Start with a fixed daily 9 AM report, then add event-triggered runs for high-value authors.

title: Jason Liu writing index: Codex and skills posts
author: Jason Liu
why_it_matters: Good ongoing source for Codex skills, evals, and personal automation patterns.
actionable_takeaway: Watch for posts that can be converted into local Codex skills or knowledge cards.

title: Codex maxxing: longer running tasks
author: OpenAI
why_it_matters: Explains the shift from quick code suggestions to delegated long-running work.
actionable_takeaway: Treat Codex as an execution partner for queued research and implementation, not only a chat helper.

title: Recommended follow candidate: OpenAI Developers
author: OpenAI Developers
why_it_matters: Developer-focused updates are more useful for Codex/GPT workflows than broad product announcements.
actionable_takeaway: Observe for 3-5 days before following; prioritize posts with docs or demos.

title: Recommended follow candidate: Simon Willison
author: Simon Willison
why_it_matters: High-signal LLM tooling, model evaluation, and practical engineering notes.
actionable_takeaway: Convert technical posts into designer-facing operation chains.

title: Recommended follow candidate: Jason Liu
author: Jason Liu
why_it_matters: Strong overlap with Codex scheduled work, skills, evals, and agent workflow.
actionable_takeaway: Track posts that translate into repeatable local Codex automations.

title: Use Claude Code / Codex in a rollback-safe sandbox and let the agent configure maximum intelligence or automation
author: Bestony / 白宦成
why_it_matters: Matches the user's need for advanced Codex productivity while preserving rollback safety.
actionable_takeaway: Investigate a local reversible sandbox profile before allowing Codex or Claude Code to tune its own config.

title: Not all automation is the same: schedule fresh tasks vs continue memoryful threads
author: Askraa.ai quoting Jason Liu
why_it_matters: Reinforces the architecture of the daily radar: fixed daily runs first, memoryful thread continuation only when needed.
actionable_takeaway: Keep this X radar as a scheduled daily job; reserve same-thread continuation for evolving investigations.

title: 10 GitHub repositories for crawling and extracting clean web data
author: 半吊子程序猿大铭
why_it_matters: Potential tool pool for AI research collection, especially pages that need JavaScript rendering or markdown extraction.
actionable_takeaway: Create a later tool-evaluation card for Firecrawl, Crawl4AI, browser-use style agents, and crawler frameworks.

title: Run coding agents from iPhone on a cloud machine; clone, edit, open PR, merge from pocket
author: blank / onealec
why_it_matters: Directly matches the user's iPhone + computer + Codex collaboration question.
actionable_takeaway: Watch this direction for concrete products that can connect iPhone command intake to cloud coding-agent execution.

title: Claude Code stack: 9 GitHub repositories layered into a small development team
author: クロニキ / chroniki_ai
why_it_matters: Shows a pattern of combining multiple repositories/tools into a repeatable AI development stack instead of relying on one app.
actionable_takeaway: Extract the stack list later and map each repo to a role: memory, UI/UX, Obsidian, LightRAG, superpowers, and workflow glue.

title: Obsidian vaults with Claude Code and AI tools as interconnected second brains
author: X trend
why_it_matters: Strong signal that local knowledge bases are becoming agent-readable operating memory, not just note archives.
actionable_takeaway: Consider making the AI radar's knowledge cards Obsidian-friendly in a future iteration.
